critiques.summary: I inspected the implemented cross-run remediation backlog code in ai_router/verification_only_app.py, its tests in ai_router/tests/test_verification_only_app.py, the schema doc docs/remediation-backlog-schema.md, and the Set 072 spec/verification artifacts. One blocking correctness gap remains in the aggregator: it reconstructs cross-run merge inputs from contributor-local fields only, so valid remediation reports that omit optional contributor severity/category can lose the per-run finding severity/category during aggregation and produce an under-ranked or miscategorized backlog entry.

findings.description:
Severity: Major
Category: correctness
Location: ai_router/verification_only_app.py:_raw_from_contributor (997-1008), aggregate_remediation_reports (1092-1138)
Violation: The set claims the cross-run backlog re-runs Set-070 merge_findings so that "MAX severity ... and verbatim contributors are identical semantics to the per-run report" and the remediation-report schema allows contributors to be only `{ surface, description, severity?, category? }` (severity/category optional). Impact: A valid remediation-report can carry its authoritative severity/category only at the merged finding level while omitting them from individual contributors; when such a report is aggregated, `_raw_from_contributor()` rebuilds raw findings from contributor fields alone, defaulting missing severity/category to `""`. That lets a Critical/Major per-run finding be re-merged as unspecified severity or blank category in the backlog, which changes severity-first ordering and can materially down-rank a defect the fixer is supposed to prioritize. Evidence: `_raw_from_contributor()` reads only `contributor.get("description")`, `contributor.get("severity")`, and `contributor.get("category")`; it never falls back to the parent finding's `severity`/`category`. The remediation-report docs explicitly mark contributor severity/category optional, and the tests only use helpers that always populate contributor severity/category, so this drift is untested. Fix: When reconstructing raw findings, fall back to the parent finding-level `severity` and `category` when the contributor omits them, and add a regression test that aggregates a valid remediation report whose contributors omit those optional fields.

critiques.summary:
I have reviewed the changes for the remediation backlog aggregator. My review focused on the new aggregation and validation logic in `ai_router/verification_only_app.py`, the corresponding tests in `ai_router/tests/test_verification_only_app.py`, and the schema documentation in `docs/remediation-backlog-schema.md`.

I can confirm the claims made in the summary. Specifically, I verified that the fix for the previously reported issue regarding `corroboration` validation was fully adopted: `validate_remediation_backlog` now correctly enforces that a finding's annotated `runs` must be distinct and be a subset of the top-level runs, and new tests (`test_validate_backlog_rejects_duplicate_run_refs`, `test_validate_backlog_rejects_stray_run_index`) confirm this. I also verified that the schema documentation was updated to reflect this stricter contract.

Additionally, I confirmed that the decision to decline the other finding (enforcing a two-way consistency check for `provenanceComplete`) is reflected in the code; the validator correctly implements only the one-way check, maintaining consistency with sibling validators as reasoned in the disposition. The implementation of the aggregation logic and its CLI is robust and well-tested. No new issues were found.
